Story: As an Admin, I want to create a new store profile with name, address, and contact details, so that the store is registered in the system and visible across the platform.
Rationale: First persistence story - absorbs the data layer (SwiftData + Supabase + sync) + base CRUD scaffolding on top of the store form. Top of scale (8); >5 for embedded infra.

Story: As an Admin, I want to assign a locale, currency, and timezone to each store, so that all transactions, reports, and communications reflect the store’s local context.
Rationale: Config fields (locale/currency/timezone) on an existing store. >1 because it affects downstream formatting/reports; <3 because it is selection + storage, no new logic.

Story: As an Admin, I want to configure payment terminals (Razorpay and card) for a new store, so that the store can accept payments from day one.
Rationale: Payment-terminal config that also stands up the payment configuration model used later by Razorpay/card. >3 for embedded setup; <8 (config, not integration).

Story: As an Admin, I want to assign a manager when creating a store, so that the store has operational ownership from day one.
Rationale: A single relational link (store->manager) via a picker. >1 because a store must have an owner (constraint); <3 because there is no workflow.

Story: As an Admin, I want to view each manager’s currently assigned store, so that I know who is responsible for which location at any time.
Rationale: Read-only lookup of each manager's current store. >1 (needs a query/join + search); <3 (display only, no logic).

Story: As an Admin, I want to view a cross-store revenue dashboard with monthly breakdowns, so that I can monitor business performance at a glance.
Rationale: Revenue dashboard - absorbs charting/visualization component setup + cross-store aggregation. Top of scale (8); >5.

Story: As a Manager, I want to create a VIP or launch event with name, date, time, venue, and description, so that the event is formally scheduled.
Rationale: Event form + scheduling + optional link to an Admin launch. >2 (relations + scheduling); <5 (standard CRUD).

Story: As a Manager, I want to send digital invitations to selected customers, so that they can be informed about upcoming events.
Rationale: Dispatch invitations over a channel, tied to an event. >1 (linked send); <3 (reuses messaging).

Story: As a Manager, I want to review shift histories so that I can monitor employee attendance.
Rationale: Read-only shift-history list per associate. >1 (query + display); <3 (no logic).

Story: As a Manager, I want to view performance metrics, units sold, upsell rate, and client satisfaction score of the sales associate so that I can evaluate employee performance.
Rationale: Aggregates units sold / upsell rate / CSAT per associate with filtering. >2 (computation over data); <5 (no ML, derived from existing records).

Story: ⁠As a Manager, I want to raise stock requests by specifying the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU), required quantity, and urgency level so that the Admin can replenish inventory through inter-store transfers or warehouse stock before products run out.
Rationale: Form (SKU/qty/urgency) + submit into the Admin queue. >2 (workflow entry); <5 (standard form).

Story: As a Manager, I want low-stock items to be flagged automatically for reorder, so that I can take action before the product runs out.
Rationale: Threshold rule + automatic flagging + dashboard surfacing. >2 (rule logic); <5 (single rule, no forecasting).

Story: As a Manager, I want to monitor the approval status of my stock requests so that I know when requested inventory will be allocated or delivered to my store.
Rationale: Status view of stock requests that updates live. >1 (live status); <3 (read-only).

Story: As a Manager, I want the system to block me from saving a price below the Admin's floor, so that I cannot breach corporate pricing policy accidentally.
Rationale: A validation guard that reuses the floor rule to block a save. >1 (enforcement point); <3 (the rule itself is defined elsewhere - TEAM5-67/19).

Story: As a Manager, I want to adjust the selling price of products within the pricing band defined by the Admin so that I can respond to local market conditions while complying with corporate pricing policies.
Rationale: Editable price clamped to the Admin band with min/max checks. >2 (validation + UI); <5 (bounded logic).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want to create a new client card with basic consented details (name, phone number, style preferences), so that I can start building a personalised shopping experience.
Rationale: Client form + consent gate that controls whether PII is persisted. >2 (conditional persistence); <5 (single form).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want to search for an existing client in the CRM by name or phone number, so that I can quickly pull up their profile when they visit.
Rationale: Search by name/phone + open profile. >1 (query + navigation); <3 (no logic).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want to book an in-store or video call appointment for a client, so that I can provide a scheduled, personalised consultation.
Rationale: In-store/video booking + reminders. >2 (two types + scheduling); <5 (no real-time media).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want to conduct a video consultation and share curated cart links with the client remotely, so that clients can shop without visiting the store.
Rationale: Real-time video consultation + remote shareable curated-cart link. >5 because real-time media + remote session is high-risk integration - top tier (8).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want to manage BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) pickup alerts, so that I can prepare the order and notify the client when it's ready for collection.
Rationale: BOPIS alert + order prep + notify-when-ready workflow. >2 (workflow + notification); <5 (bounded).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want to create ship-from-warehouse orders for remote selling, so that products not available in-store can be shipped directly to the client.
Rationale: Order created against warehouse stock + shipping details. >2 (cross-location order); <5 (standard order flow).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want the system to generate product recommendations based on a client's purchase patterns and preferences, so that I have data-driven suggestions to present.
Rationale: AI recommendations - Core ML model + inference pipeline + data prep (first ML subsystem). Top of scale (8) due to tech-stack difficulty; >5.

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want the system to suggest trending and similar products for new clients with no purchase history, so that I can still offer relevant options.
Rationale: Cold-start trending/similar fallback for new clients. >2 (model/heuristic); <5 (simpler fallback than the full pattern engine).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want the system to surface complementary products at the cart stage, so that I can suggest cross-sell options to the client.
Rationale: Surface complementary items at the cart stage. >2 (cart-context logic); <5 (reuses recommendation engine).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want the system to suggest higher-value alternatives at the cart stage, so that I can offer upsell options that match the client's taste.
Rationale: Surface higher-value alternatives at the cart stage. >2 (cart-context logic); <5 (reuses recommendation engine).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want to check inventory availability (in-store, warehouse, nearby stores) before presenting a product, so that I don't recommend items that are out of stock.
Rationale: Real-time availability across in-store/warehouse/nearby. >2 (multi-source query); <5 (read aggregation).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want to scan a product's QR code at the point of sale, so that both the client's purchase history and the store's inventory records are updated simultaneously in one action.
Rationale: QR sale dual-update - first Vision/QR scanner infra + atomic inventory+history transaction + concurrency. Top of scale (8); >5.

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want the system to suggest similar alternative products when the desired item is unavailable, so that I can still serve the client without losing the sale.
Rationale: Suggest alternatives when the item is unavailable. >2 (conditional path); <5 (reuses recommendations).

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want to take payment via Razorpay and issue a digital receipt, so that checkout is fast and compliant.
Rationale: Razorpay - external payment SDK + compliance + digital receipt (first payment subsystem). Top of scale (8); >5.

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want to process payments via card terminal (tap/swipe), so that clients can pay with credit or debit cards.
Rationale: Card-terminal (tap/swipe) integration capturing the result. >2 (hardware integration); kept <5/8 because result handling is more bounded than the Razorpay SDK flow.

Story: As a Sales Associate, I want to route the completed order as a store sale, BOPIS reservation, or ship-from-warehouse, so that the correct fulfilment process is triggered based on the order type.
Rationale: Branch the completed order into 3 fulfilment flows. >2 (multi-path routing); <5 (each path defined elsewhere).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to scan a product's QR code to look up the item, so that I can quickly identify the product and pull its records.
Rationale: Vision QR scan + record retrieval. >2 (scan + lookup); <5 (scan infra reused from sales).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to manually enter product details if the QR scan fails, so that I can still create a ticket for items with damaged labels.
Rationale: Simple manual-entry form when the scan fails. >1 (alternate path); <3 (basic form).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to pull the client's purchase history linked to the product, so that I have full context on the original transaction.
Rationale: Lookup purchase record by serial + display. >1 (linked query); <3 (read only).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to capture condition photos and a diagnostic report in-app at intake, so that the item's state is documented before any work begins.
Rationale: In-app condition-photo capture + diagnostics stored with the ticket. >2 (media capture + storage); <5 (no processing).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to create an after-sales ticket (AST) tagged with the correct service type (exchange, return, repair, warranty), so that the ticket follows the right workflow.
Rationale: AST creation whose service type drives the downstream workflow. >2 (type-driven routing); <5 (form + branch).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to validate whether the product's warranty is still active, so that I can flag it as a warranty repair (free) or a paid repair.
Rationale: Serial lookup -> warranty status -> free/paid branch. >2 (lookup + decision); <5 (single rule).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want the system to flag out-of-warranty or voided coverage clearly, so that the client is informed upfront about potential costs.
Rationale: Coverage status indicator + paid-repair flag. >1 (derived flag/UI); <3 (display).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to track a repair through defined stages, so that the progress is transparent and auditable.
Rationale: Repair state machine with timestamped transitions. >2 (ordered stages); <5 (bounded states).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want a mandatory QA checklist to be completed before marking a repair as done, so that no item is returned to the client without quality verification.
Rationale: Gate that blocks completion until the QA checklist is fully done. >2 (gating logic); <5 (single rule).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to send items back to repair if they fail the QA check, so that issues are resolved before the client receives the product.
Rationale: Single transition returning the item to repair + reason log. >1 (transition + log); <3 (one step).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to schedule a store pickup or courier for the completed item, so that the client can conveniently collect or receive their product.
Rationale: Scheduling with pickup or courier options. >2 (two methods); <5 (bounded).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to generate return documentation for the completed service, so that there is a formal record of the handover.
Rationale: Generate a return document attached to the AST. >1 (doc generation); <3 (templated).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want the client to digitally sign off on collection, so that we have proof of delivery and the ticket can be formally closed.
Rationale: Digital signature capture stored against the ticket. >2 (signature handling); <5 (single capture).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to scan a final packaging QR before releasing the item, so that inventory records are updated and the AST is closed accurately.
Rationale: Packaging QR scan gate before release + close + inventory sync. >2 (gate + sync); <5 (reuses scan).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to generate an itemised cost estimate (parts + labour + timeline), so that the client knows exactly what the repair will cost and how long it will take.
Rationale: Compute an itemised estimate (parts + labour + timeline). >2 (computation + breakdown); <5 (form + calc).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want to send the cost estimate to the client digitally for approval or decline, so that no work begins without the client's consent.
Rationale: Secure link to the client + record approve/decline. >2 (link issuance + decision capture); <5 (reuses link infra).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want the repair to proceed only after client approval, so that we avoid disputes over unauthorised work.
Rationale: Block the repair from advancing until approval is recorded. >2 (gating); <5 (single rule).

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist, I want declined repairs to skip the repair workflow and go directly to return/pickup scheduling, so that the client can retrieve their item without delay.
Rationale: Routing rule sending declined repairs straight to pickup. >1 (conditional route); <3 (one branch).

Story: As an Admin, I want the manager profile view to be read-only, so that performance records cannot be altered.
Rationale: Pure display with edit controls removed - the simplest possible item, hence 1 (nothing below it).

Story: As an Admin, I want to log in with my credentials, so that I can access only the features and data relevant to my role.
Rationale: Bootstraps the app - Passkey + RBAC framework + scaffold (MVVM/DI/navigation) + Keychain - on top of the login feature. Top of the scale (8); true effort exceeds a typical 8 but the scale is capped at 8.

Story: As a Manager, I want to log in with my credentials, so that I can access only the features and data relevant to my role.
Rationale: Reuses the auth framework built in TEAM5-60; only role-scoping/routing differs. 2 - lighter than a standard feature.

Story: As a Sales Associate I want to log in with my credentials, so that I can access only the features and data relevant to my role.
Rationale: Reuses the auth framework from TEAM5-60; only role-scoping differs. 2 - lighter than a standard feature.

Story: As an After-Sales Specialist I want to log in with my credentials, so that I can access only the features and data relevant to my role.
Rationale: Reuses the auth framework from TEAM5-60; only role-scoping differs. 2 - lighter than a standard feature.

Story: As an Admin, I want to filter the revenue dashboard by country, so that I can analyse performance across specific geographies.
Rationale: A country filter on an existing dashboard. >1 (recompute + UI control); <3 (no new data or logic).

Story: As an Admin, I want to create a new SKU with product details and define its launch date, so that the product is catalogued and ready for distribution.
Rationale: SKU form + uniqueness + launch-date validation + cataloguing. >2 for the rules; <5 because asset push lives in a separate concern.

Story: As an Admin, I want to set a minimum floor price per SKU per currency, so that managers cannot discount below a level that protects brand value and margins.
Rationale: Per-currency price-band data model + floor constraint enforced downstream. >2 for the model + rule; <5 because enforcement logic sits in the pricing service.

Story: As an Admin, I want to receive and review transfer requests raised by managers, so that I can evaluate stock needs across the network.
Rationale: Queue/list of transfer requests with sort. >1 (query + display); <3 (no decision logic - that is a separate story).

Story: As a Manager, I want to track RSVP responses (Accepted, Declined, Pending), so that I can estimate event attendance.
Rationale: RSVP status capture per invitee + counts. >1 (per-invitee state); <3 (simple tracking).

Story: As a Manager, I want to view event analytics and RSVP reports, so that I can evaluate event performance.
Rationale: Aggregated invites/RSVP/attendance report + export. >2 (aggregation + export); <5 (reporting on existing data).

Story: As an Admin I want to remove store profile so that the store which is not operational doesn't reflect in database.
Rationale: Soft-delete + confirmation + retain history + hide from active lists. >2 (more than a delete button); <5 (contained scope).

Story: As an Admin I want to delete Manager profile so that the person who has resgined is not in the database anymore.
Rationale: Soft-delete + audit on a single entity. >1 (confirmation/audit); <3 (single record, no cascade workflow).

Story: As a Manager I want to delete Staff profile so that the person who has resgined is not in the database anymore.
Rationale: Soft-delete + audit of a staff record. >1 (confirmation/audit); <3 (single entity).

Story: As an After-Sales Associate, I want to verify a product's authenticity so that counterfeit items are not accepted for service.
Rationale: Serial/certificate authenticity verification + counterfeit flag. >3 (cross-checks beyond a lookup); <8 (no external/ML verification).

Story: As an After-Sales Associate, I want to view a product's service history so that I can understand previous repairs before creating a new request.
Rationale: Service history by serial with prior outcomes. >2 (linked history); <5 (read aggregation).

Story: As an After-Sales Associate, I want to view pending customer approvals so that I can follow up on delayed responses.
Rationale: Pending-approvals list with an age indicator. >1 (filtered list + derived field); <3 (read only).

Story: As an After-Sales Associate, I want to generate invoices for completed repairs so that customers receive a detailed breakdown of charges.
Rationale: Itemised invoice for completed paid repairs. >2 (computation + document); <5 (templated).

Story: As an After-Sales Associate, I want to filter repair requests by status so that I can quickly locate specific service cases.
Rationale: Filter repair requests by status. >1 (filter control); <3 (list view).

Story: As an After-Sales Associate, I want to view all active service requests so that I can monitor ongoing repair workloads.
Rationale: List active service requests with stage/assignee. >1 (query + display); <3 (read only).

Story: As an Admin, I want to verify warehouse stock availability for requested items so that I can decide whether inventory can be transferred immediately.
Rationale: On-hand lookup per location + shortfall flag. >1 (query + indicator); <3 (no decision/routing here).

Story: As an Admin, I want to approve transfer requests when sufficient stock is available so that inventory can be dispatched to stores.
Rationale: Approval that checks stock and routes from a source store. >2 (conditional logic + state change); <5 (no multi-store optimisation).

Story: As an Admin, I want to create purchase orders when warehouse stock is insufficient so that required inventory can be replenished.
Rationale: PO form + supplier, triggered when stock is insufficient. >2 (trigger condition + form); <5 (standard CRUD).

Story: As an Admin, I want to dispatch approved inventory transfers to stores so that requested products reach their destination.
Rationale: State transition + source decrement + in-transit marking. >2 (inventory mutation logic); <5 (single, well-defined transition).

Story: As an Admin, I want store managers to receive transfer status notifications so that they can prepare for incoming inventory.
Rationale: First notification story - absorbs the push/SMS notification infrastructure. >2 because the messaging pipeline is built here; later notifications reuse it.
